description: Running a global build/test command for Swift instead of swift build / xcodebuild via SPM misses the project-local config and reports misleading results.
body: Failure mode: a generic root-level command is run for Swift work instead of swift build / xcodebuild via SPM, so project-local configuration, lockfiles, and scripts are ignored and the output does not reflect the real build. Detection signals: the command ignores the project manifest; results differ from CI; tooling versions or flags do not match the repo. Do not run a global or default command when the project declares its own Swift toolchain. Safer fallback: detect and run swift build / xcodebuild via SPM from the project root, verify the version matches the repo, and record the exact command and exit code.
